task_name: Define Live Builder Adapter Readiness Contract
goal: Define the Live Builder Adapter Readiness boundary after Builder Task Create Readiness without enabling live Builder calls, task creation, execution, auth, secrets, persistence, approval recording, DB, deploy, approve, or push.
claims: BP-072 defines a Live Builder Adapter Readiness contract.
BP-072 keeps live Builder calls, task creation, execution, auth, secrets, persistence, approval recording, DB, deploy, approve, and push blocked.
BP-072 does not change runtime behavior, fixtures, packages, app code, or external systems.
assumptions: BP-071 documented the local MVP chain through Builder Task Create Readiness Candidate.
A Live Builder Adapter Readiness boundary is needed before any future live adapter implementation or task creation work.
The existing Builder Live Read Probe remains a mock/read-only precedent, not permission for live mutation.
dod: The document defines purpose, input envelope, output envelope, hard gates, LLM-free deterministic gates, human review triggers, blocked actions, fail behavior, and next safe step.
The document explicitly blocks live Builder calls, Builder Task Create, Execute, Approve, Push, Deploy, auth, secrets, persistence, approval recording, DB, UI, and target file writes.
stop_conditions: Runtime, fixture, package, or tool changes become necessary
Live Builder access, task creation, execution, approval recording, auth implementation, secrets, persistence, DB, deploy, approve, or push becomes necessary
prior_task_findings: BP-071 documented the local MVP chain through Builder Task Create Readiness and recommended a Live Builder Adapter Readiness contract as the next safe boundary.
